knowledgeChunks.summary: Standard Next.js configuration file for the 'web' package in a monorepo. It exports a minimal NextConfig object with only React Strict Mode enabled, using TypeScript for type safety.

Entry point for the 'brain' CLI tool (BrainShield), which uses Commander.js to define and register subcommands (brain, chat, fix, report, config, init) for AI-powered security analysis. It conditionally prints a banner unless machine-readable output is expected (--json or --version flags), and defaults to running the 'brain' scan command when invoked without a subcommand.

This file implements Next.js middleware using Clerk authentication to protect routes matching the `/dashboard` path pattern. It uses Clerk's `createRouteMatcher` to define protected routes and calls `auth.protect()` to enforce authentication on matched requests. The middleware matcher config ensures it runs on all routes except static assets and Next.js internals.

Defines a Commander.js CLI subcommand `config` for managing BrainShield configuration settings, supporting operations to set an Anthropic API key, set a Claude model, and display the current configuration (API key, model, max cost, config path) with styled terminal output using chalk. It persists settings via an imported config store and provides guidance when no API key is configured.

Defines a persistent configuration store for the BrainShield CLI using the `conf` library, with typed settings for Anthropic API key, model selection (defaulting to claude-opus-4-6), and scan cost/token limits. Provides a `getApiKey()` helper that resolves the Anthropic API key from the environment variable first, falling back to the stored configuration value.

This is the root layout component for a Next.js application that wraps the entire app with Clerk authentication provider and sets a default dark theme. It exports static metadata configuration for SEO, Open Graph tags, and site identity for the BrainShield AI security CLI web dashboard.

This file implements an authentication and session security scanner agent that uses regex-based pattern matching to detect common auth vulnerabilities (e.g., weak JWT verification, hardcoded secrets, missing CSRF protection, insecure cookie settings, localStorage token storage) across project source files. Detected findings are deduplicated, and when there are 15 or fewer results, they are enriched via a Claude LLM call that confirms or rejects each finding as a false positive and adjusts severity and fix recommendations accordingly.

A minimal Next.js layout component for the marketing route group `(marketing)`. It acts as a pass-through layout that renders its children without adding any additional wrapping elements or shared UI, using a React Fragment.

This is a Next.js layout component for the dashboard section that sets page metadata and renders a flex container with a sidebar navigation component and a main content area. It uses a dark background color (#09090b) and ensures the layout fills the full viewport height while allowing the main content to scroll independently.

This is a Next.js sign-in page component that renders a Clerk `<SignIn>` widget with a custom dark-themed appearance using Tailwind CSS classes. It features a branded BrainShield logo header with a violet accent color scheme, a decorative background glow effect, and a link back to the home page. The catch-all route segment `[[...sign-in]]` allows Clerk to handle its multi-step authentication flows.

This is a Next.js sign-up page component that renders Clerk's `<SignUp>` authentication widget with a custom dark-themed appearance using Tailwind CSS. It features the BrainShield branding with a shield icon, a decorative violet gradient background blur effect, and a link back to the home page. The file uses Next.js catch-all route segments (`[[...sign-up]]`) to handle Clerk's multi-step sign-up flow.
